briefdescription.para: distance map from given polyline with values offset

detaileddescription.para: distance map from shell of given polyline (perEdgeOffset should not have negative values )

detect sign of distance based on closest contour's edge turn (recommended for good contours with no self-intersections)
that polyline topology should be consistently oriented
detect sign of distance based on number of ray intersections with contours (recommended for contours with self-intersections)

Structure with parameters to generate DistanceMap by Contours Generated from class MR::ContourToDistanceMapParams. Supported MR_PassBy modes: MR_PassBy_DefaultConstruct, MR_PassBy_Copy, MR_PassBy_Move (and MR_PassBy_DefaultArgument and MR_PassBy_NoObject if supported by the callee).

Row-major matrix with T values Generated from class MR::Matrix<float>. Base classes: Direct: (non-virtual) MR::RectIndexer Supported MR_PassBy modes: MR_PassBy_DefaultConstruct, MR_PassBy_Copy, MR_PassBy_Move (and MR_PassBy_DefaultArgument and MR_PassBy_NoObject if supported by the callee).

Generated from class MR::MeshToDistanceMapParams. Supported MR_PassBy modes: MR_PassBy_DefaultConstruct, MR_PassBy_Copy, MR_PassBy_Move (and MR_PassBy_DefaultArgument and MR_PassBy_NoObject if supported by the callee).

a class for converting 2D integer coordinates into 1D linear coordinates and backward Generated from class MR::RectIndexer. Derived classes: Direct: (non-virtual) MR::DistanceMap MR::Matrix<float> Supported MR_PassBy modes: MR_PassBy_DefaultConstruct, MR_PassBy_Copy, MR_PassBy_Move (and MR_PassBy_DefaultArgument and MR_PassBy_NoObject if supported by the callee).

Structure with parameters for optional offset in distanceMapFromContours function Generated from class MR::ContoursDistanceMapOffset. Supported MR_PassBy modes: MR_PassBy_Copy, MR_PassBy_Move (and MR_PassBy_DefaultArgument and MR_PassBy_NoObject if supported by the callee).

Generated from class MR::ContoursDistanceMapOptions. Supported MR_PassBy modes: MR_PassBy_DefaultConstruct, MR_PassBy_Copy, MR_PassBy_Move (and MR_PassBy_DefaultArgument and MR_PassBy_NoObject if supported by the callee).

a constant that is treated as 'no value' or 'invalid value' Returns a pointer to a member variable of class MR::DistanceMap named NOT_VALID_VALUE. The returned pointer will never be null. It is non-owning, do NOT destroy it.
Constructs an empty (default-constructed) instance. Never returns null. Returns an instance allocated on the heap! Must call MR_DistanceMap_Destroy() to free it when you're done using it.

Constructs an array of empty (default-constructed) instances, of the specified size. Will never return null. The array must be destroyed using MR_DistanceMap_DestroyArray(). Use MR_DistanceMap_OffsetMutablePtr() and MR_DistanceMap_OffsetPtr() to access the array elements.

Offsets a pointer to an array element by i positions (not bytes). Use only if you're certain that the pointer points to an array element. The reference to the parameter ptr might be preserved in the return value.

Offsets a pointer to an array element by i positions (not bytes). Use only if you're certain that the pointer points to an array element. The reference to the parameter ptr might be preserved in the return value.
Upcasts an instance of MR::DistanceMap to its base class MR::RectIndexer. This version is acting on mutable pointers. The reference to the parameter object might be preserved in the return value.
Upcasts an instance of MR::DistanceMap to its base class MR::RectIndexer. The reference to the parameter object might be preserved in the return value.

Downcasts an instance of MR::RectIndexer to a derived class MR::DistanceMap. This is a static downcast, it trusts the programmer that the target type is correct. Results in UB and returns an invalid pointer otherwise. This version is acting on mutable pointers. The reference to the parameter object might be preserved in the return value.

Downcasts an instance of MR::RectIndexer to a derived class MR::DistanceMap. This is a static downcast, it trusts the programmer that the target type is correct. Results in UB and returns an invalid pointer otherwise. The reference to the parameter object might be preserved in the return value.

Generated from constructor MR::DistanceMap::DistanceMap. The reference to things referred to by the parameter _other (if any) might be preserved in the constructed object. Never returns null. Returns an instance allocated on the heap! Must call MR_DistanceMap_Destroy() to free it when you're done using it.

Preferable constructor with resolution arguments Access by the index (i) is equal to (y*resX + x) Generated from constructor MR::DistanceMap::DistanceMap. The reference to the parameter resX might be preserved in the constructed object. The reference to the parameter resY might be preserved in the constructed object. Never returns null. Returns an instance allocated on the heap! Must call MR_DistanceMap_Destroy() to free it when you're done using it.

make from 2d array Generated from constructor MR::DistanceMap::DistanceMap. Parameter m can not be null. It is a single object. The reference to the parameter m might be preserved in the constructed object. Never returns null. Returns an instance allocated on the heap! Must call MR_DistanceMap_Destroy() to free it when you're done using it.

briefdescription.para: Destroys a heap-allocated instance of MR_DistanceMap. Does nothing if the pointer is null.
Destroys a heap-allocated array of MR_DistanceMap. Does nothing if the pointer is null.

detaileddescription.para: Generated from method MR::DistanceMap::operator=. Parameter _this can not be null. It is a single object. The reference to things referred to by the parameter _other (if any) might be preserved in this object. The returned pointer will never be null. It is non-owning, do NOT destroy it. When this function is called, this object will drop any object references it held previously.
checks if X,Y element is valid (i.e. not NOT_VALID_VALUE; passing invalid coords to this is UB) Generated from method MR::DistanceMap::isValid. Parameter _this can not be null. It is a single object.

checks if index element is valid (i.e. not NOT_VALID_VALUE; passing an invalid coord to this is UB) Generated from method MR::DistanceMap::isValid. Parameter _this can not be null. It is a single object.
Returns true if (X,Y) coordinates are in bounds. Generated from method MR::DistanceMap::isInBounds. Parameter _this can not be null. It is a single object.
Returns true if a flattened coordinate is in bounds. Generated from method MR::DistanceMap::isInBounds. Parameter _this can not be null. It is a single object.

returns value in (X,Y) element, returns nullopt if not valid (see isValid()), UB if out of bounds. Generated from method MR::DistanceMap::get. Parameter _this can not be null. It is a single object. Never returns null. Returns an instance allocated on the heap! Must call MR_std_optional_float_Destroy() to free it when you're done using it.

returns value of index element, returns nullopt if not valid (see isValid()), UB if out of bounds. Generated from method MR::DistanceMap::get. Parameter _this can not be null. It is a single object. Never returns null. Returns an instance allocated on the heap! Must call MR_std_optional_float_Destroy() to free it when you're done using it.

returns value in (X,Y) element without check on valid use this only if you sure that distance map has no invalid values or for serialization Generated from method MR::DistanceMap::getValue. Parameter _this can not be null. It is a single object. The returned pointer will never be null. It is non-owning, do NOT destroy it.
Generated from method MR::DistanceMap::getValue. Parameter _this can not be null. It is a single object.
Generated from method MR::DistanceMap::getValue. Parameter _this can not be null. It is a single object. The returned pointer will never be null. It is non-owning, do NOT destroy it.
Generated from method MR::DistanceMap::getValue. Parameter _this can not be null. It is a single object.
Generated from method MR::DistanceMap::data. Parameter _this can not be null. It is a single object.
Generated from method MR::DistanceMap::data. Parameter _this can not be null. It is a single object.

https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bilinear_interpolation getInterpolated( 0.5f, 0.5f ) == get( 0, 0 ) see https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/windows/win32/direct3d10/d3d10-graphics-programming-guide-resources-coordinates for details all 4 elements around this point should be valid, returns nullopt if at least one is not valid
x y
should be in resolution range [0;resX][0;resY]. If x,y are out of bounds, returns nullopt. Generated from method MR::DistanceMap::getInterpolated. Parameter _this can not be null. It is a single object. Never returns null. Returns an instance allocated on the heap! Must call MR_std_optional_float_Destroy() to free it when you're done using it.

memberdef.argsstring: (const MR_DistanceMap *_this, MR_uint64_t x, MR_uint64_t y, const MR_AffineXf3f *toWorld)
memberdef.name: MR_DistanceMap_unproject

detaileddescription.para: finds 3d coordinates of the Point on the model surface for the (x,y) pixel Use the same params with distance map creation (x,y) must be in bounds, the behavior is undefined otherwise. Generated from method MR::DistanceMap::unproject. Parameter _this can not be null. It is a single object. Parameter toWorld can not be null. It is a single object. Never returns null. Returns an instance allocated on the heap! Must call MR_std_optional_MR_Vector3f_Destroy() to free it when you're done using it.

memberdef.argsstring: (const MR_DistanceMap *_this, float x, float y, const MR_AffineXf3f *toWorld)
memberdef.name: MR_DistanceMap_unprojectInterpolated

briefdescription.para: finds 3d coordinates of the Point on the model surface for the (x,y) interpolated value

detaileddescription.para:
x y
should be in resolution range [0;resX][0;resY].
getInterpolated( 0.5f, 0.5f ) == get( 0, 0 ) see https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/windows/win32/direct3d10/d3d10-graphics-programming-guide-resources-coordinates for details all 4 elements around this point should be valid, returns nullopt if at least one is not valid If x,y are out of bounds, returns nullopt. Generated from method MR::DistanceMap::unprojectInterpolated. Parameter _this can not be null. It is a single object. Parameter toWorld can not be null. It is a single object. Never returns null. Returns an instance allocated on the heap! Must call MR_std_optional_MR_Vector3f_Destroy() to free it when you're done using it.
replaces every valid element in the map with its negative value Generated from method MR::DistanceMap::negate. Parameter _this can not be null. It is a single object.

boolean operators returns new Distance Map with cell-wise maximum values. Invalid values remain only if both corresponding cells are invalid Generated from method MR::DistanceMap::max. Parameter _this can not be null. It is a single object. Parameter rhs can not be null. It is a single object. Never returns null. Returns an instance allocated on the heap! Must call MR_DistanceMap_Destroy() to free it when you're done using it.

replaces values with cell-wise maximum values. Invalid values remain only if both corresponding cells are invalid Generated from method MR::DistanceMap::mergeMax. Parameter _this can not be null. It is a single object. Parameter rhs can not be null. It is a single object. The returned pointer will never be null. It is non-owning, do NOT destroy it.

returns new Distance Map with cell-wise minimum values. Invalid values remain only if both corresponding cells are invalid Generated from method MR::DistanceMap::min. Parameter _this can not be null. It is a single object. Parameter rhs can not be null. It is a single object. Never returns null. Returns an instance allocated on the heap! Must call MR_DistanceMap_Destroy() to free it when you're done using it.

replaces values with cell-wise minimum values. Invalid values remain only if both corresponding cells are invalid Generated from method MR::DistanceMap::mergeMin. Parameter _this can not be null. It is a single object. Parameter rhs can not be null. It is a single object. The returned pointer will never be null. It is non-owning, do NOT destroy it.

returns new Distance Map with cell-wise subtracted values. Invalid values remain only if both corresponding cells are invalid Generated from method MR::DistanceMap::operator-. Parameter _this can not be null. It is a single object. Parameter rhs can not be null. It is a single object. Never returns null. Returns an instance allocated on the heap! Must call MR_DistanceMap_Destroy() to free it when you're done using it.

replaces values with cell-wise subtracted values. Invalid values remain only if both corresponding cells are invalid Generated from method MR::DistanceMap::operator-=. Parameter _this can not be null. It is a single object. Parameter rhs can not be null. It is a single object. The returned pointer will never be null. It is non-owning, do NOT destroy it.

memberdef.name: MR_DistanceMap_set_3

detaileddescription.para: sets value in (X,Y) element (the coords must be valid, UB otherwise) Generated from method MR::DistanceMap::set. Parameter _this can not be null. It is a single object.
sets value in index element (the coord must be valid, UB otherwise) Generated from method MR::DistanceMap::set. Parameter _this can not be null. It is a single object.
sets all values at one time Generated from method MR::DistanceMap::set. Parameter _this can not be null. It is a single object.
invalidates value in (X,Y) element (the coords must be valid, UB otherwise) Generated from method MR::DistanceMap::unset. Parameter _this can not be null. It is a single object.
invalidates value in index element (the coord must be valid, UB otherwise) Generated from method MR::DistanceMap::unset. Parameter _this can not be null. It is a single object.
invalidates all elements Generated from method MR::DistanceMap::invalidateAll. Parameter _this can not be null. It is a single object.
clears data, sets resolutions to zero Generated from method MR::DistanceMap::clear. Parameter _this can not be null. It is a single object.

returns new derivatives map without directions Generated from method MR::DistanceMap::getDerivativeMap. Parameter _this can not be null. It is a single object. Never returns null. Returns an instance allocated on the heap! Must call MR_DistanceMap_Destroy() to free it when you're done using it.

returns new derivative maps with X and Y axes direction Generated from method MR::DistanceMap::getXYDerivativeMaps. Parameter _this can not be null. It is a single object. Never returns null. Returns an instance allocated on the heap! Must call MR_std_pair_MR_DistanceMap_MR_DistanceMap_Destroy() to free it when you're done using it.

computes single derivative map from XY spaces combined. Returns local maximums then Generated from method MR::DistanceMap::getLocalMaximums. Parameter _this can not be null. It is a single object. Never returns null. Returns an instance allocated on the heap! Must call MR_std_vector_std_pair_uint64_t_uint64_t_Destroy() to free it when you're done using it.
returns X resolution Generated from method MR::DistanceMap::resX. Parameter _this can not be null. It is a single object.
returns Y resolution Generated from method MR::DistanceMap::resY. Parameter _this can not be null. It is a single object.
returns the number of pixels Generated from method MR::DistanceMap::numPoints. Parameter _this can not be null. It is a single object.

finds minimum and maximum values returns min_float and max_float if all values are invalid Generated from method MR::DistanceMap::getMinMaxValues. Parameter _this can not be null. It is a single object. Never returns null. Returns an instance allocated on the heap! Must call MR_std_pair_float_float_Destroy() to free it when you're done using it.

finds minimum value X,Y returns [-1.-1] if all values are invalid Generated from method MR::DistanceMap::getMinIndex. Parameter _this can not be null. It is a single object. Never returns null. Returns an instance allocated on the heap! Must call MR_std_pair_uint64_t_uint64_t_Destroy() to free it when you're done using it.

finds maximum value X,Y returns [-1.-1] if all values are invalid Generated from method MR::DistanceMap::getMaxIndex. Parameter _this can not be null. It is a single object. Never returns null. Returns an instance allocated on the heap! Must call MR_std_pair_uint64_t_uint64_t_Destroy() to free it when you're done using it.
returns the amount of memory this object occupies on heap Generated from method MR::DistanceMap::heapBytes. Parameter _this can not be null. It is a single object.
Generated from method MR::DistanceMap::resize. Parameter _this can not be null. It is a single object. Parameter dims can not be null. It is a single object.
Generated from method MR::DistanceMap::dims. Parameter _this can not be null. It is a single object. The returned pointer will never be null. It is non-owning, do NOT destroy it.

fill another distance map pair with gradients across X and Y axes of the argument map Generated from function MR::combineXYderivativeMaps. Never returns null. Returns an instance allocated on the heap! Must call MR_DistanceMap_Destroy() to free it when you're done using it.

memberdef.argsstring: (const MR_MeshPart *mp, const MR_MeshToDistanceMapParams *params, MR_PassBy cb_pass_by, MR_std_function_bool_from_float *cb, MR_std_vector_MR_MeshTriPoint *outSamples)
memberdef.name: MR_computeDistanceMap

detaileddescription.para: computes distance (height) map for given projection parameters using float-precision for finding ray-mesh intersections, which is faster but less reliable Generated from function MR::computeDistanceMap. Parameter mp can not be null. It is a single object. Parameter params can not be null. It is a single object. Parameter cb has a default argument: {}, pass MR_PassBy_DefaultArgument and a null pointer to use it. Parameter outSamples defaults to a null pointer in C++. Never returns null. Returns an instance allocated on the heap! Must call MR_DistanceMap_Destroy() to free it when you're done using it.

memberdef.argsstring: (const MR_MeshPart *mp, const MR_MeshToDistanceMapParams *params, MR_PassBy cb_pass_by, MR_std_function_bool_from_float *cb, MR_std_vector_MR_MeshTriPoint *outSamples)
memberdef.name: MR_computeDistanceMapD

detaileddescription.para: computes distance (height) map for given projection parameters using double-precision for finding ray-mesh intersections, which is slower but more reliable Generated from function MR::computeDistanceMapD. Parameter mp can not be null. It is a single object. Parameter params can not be null. It is a single object. Parameter cb has a default argument: {}, pass MR_PassBy_DefaultArgument and a null pointer to use it. Parameter outSamples defaults to a null pointer in C++. Never returns null. Returns an instance allocated on the heap! Must call MR_DistanceMap_Destroy() to free it when you're done using it.

offset values for each undirected edge of given polyline Returns a pointer to a member variable of class MR::ContoursDistanceMapOffset named perEdgeOffset. Parameter _this can not be null. It is a single object. The returned pointer will never be null. It is non-owning, do NOT destroy it. The reference to this object might be preserved as the return value.

The reference to the parameter value might be preserved in this object in element type. When this function is called, this object will drop object references it held previously in type.

Constructs an empty (default-constructed) instance. Never returns null. Returns an instance allocated on the heap! Must call MR_ContoursDistanceMapOptions_Destroy() to free it when you're done using it.

Constructs an array of empty (default-constructed) instances, of the specified size. Will never return null. The array must be destroyed using MR_ContoursDistanceMapOptions_DestroyArray(). Use MR_ContoursDistanceMapOptions_OffsetMutablePtr() and MR_ContoursDistanceMapOptions_OffsetPtr() to access the array elements.

detaileddescription.para: Constructs MR::ContoursDistanceMapOptions elementwise. The reference to the parameter signMethod might be preserved in the constructed object. The reference to the parameter offsetParameters might be preserved in the constructed object. The reference to the parameter region might be preserved in the constructed object. The reference to the parameter outClosestEdges might be preserved in the constructed object. Never returns null. Returns an instance allocated on the heap! Must call MR_ContoursDistanceMapOptions_Destroy() to free it when you're done using it. When this function is called, this object will drop any object references it held previously.

Offsets a pointer to an array element by i positions (not bytes). Use only if you're certain that the pointer points to an array element. The reference to the parameter ptr might be preserved in the return value.

detaileddescription.para: Generated from method MR::ContoursDistanceMapOptions::operator=. Parameter _this can not be null. It is a single object. Parameter _other can not be null. It is a single object. The reference to things referred to by the parameter _other (if any) might be preserved in this object. The returned pointer will never be null. It is non-owning, do NOT destroy it. When this function is called, this object will drop any object references it held previously.

briefdescription.para: Computes distance of 2d contours according ContourToDistanceMapParams.

parameterdescription.para: - optional input and output options for distance map calculation, find more ContoursDistanceMapOptions Generated from function MR::distanceMapFromContours. Parameter contours can not be null. It is a single object. Parameter params can not be null. It is a single object. Parameter options is a single object. Parameter options has a default argument: {}, pass a null pointer to use it. Never returns null. Returns an instance allocated on the heap! Must call MR_DistanceMap_Destroy() to free it when you're done using it.

memberdef.argsstring: (MR_DistanceMap *distMap, const MR_Polyline2 *polyline, const MR_ContourToDistanceMapParams *params, const MR_ContoursDistanceMapOptions *options)
memberdef.name: MR_distanceMapFromContours_4

briefdescription.para: Computes distance of 2d contours according ContourToDistanceMapParams.

parameterdescription.para: - optional input and output options for distance map calculation, find more ContoursDistanceMapOptions Generated from function MR::distanceMapFromContours. Parameter distMap can not be null. It is a single object. Parameter polyline can not be null. It is a single object. Parameter params can not be null. It is a single object. Parameter options is a single object. Parameter options has a default argument: {}, pass a null pointer to use it.

detaileddescription.para:
Makes distance map and filter out pixels with large (>threshold) distance between closest points on contour in neighbor pixels Converts such points back in 3d space and return
that polyline topology should be consistently oriented Generated from function MR::edgePointsFromContours. Parameter polyline can not be null. It is a single object. Never returns null. Returns an instance allocated on the heap! Must call MR_std_vector_MR_Vector3f_Destroy() to free it when you're done using it.

converts distance map to 2d iso-lines: iso-lines are created in space DistanceMap ( plane OXY with pixelSize = (1, 1) ) Generated from function MR::distanceMapTo2DIsoPolyline. Parameter distMap can not be null. It is a single object. Never returns null. Returns an instance allocated on the heap! Must call MR_Polyline2_Destroy() to free it when you're done using it.

iso-lines are created in real space ( plane OXY with parameters according ContourToDistanceMapParams ) Generated from function MR::distanceMapTo2DIsoPolyline. Parameter distMap can not be null. It is a single object. Parameter params can not be null. It is a single object. Never returns null. Returns an instance allocated on the heap! Must call MR_Polyline2_Destroy() to free it when you're done using it.

memberdef.argsstring: (const MR_DistanceMap *distMap, const MR_AffineXf3f *xf, float isoValue, const bool *useDepth)
memberdef.name: MR_distanceMapTo2DIsoPolyline_4

detaileddescription.para: Generated from function MR::distanceMapTo2DIsoPolyline. Parameter distMap can not be null. It is a single object. Never returns null. Returns an instance allocated on the heap! Must call MR_Polyline2_Destroy() to free it when you're done using it.

constructs an offset contour for given polyline Generated from function MR::polylineOffset. Parameter polyline can not be null. It is a single object. Never returns null. Returns an instance allocated on the heap! Must call MR_Polyline2_Destroy() to free it when you're done using it.

memberdef.argsstring: (const MR_Polyline2 *contoursA, const MR_Polyline2 *contoursB, const MR_ContourToDistanceMapParams *params, const float *offsetInside)
memberdef.name: MR_contourUnion

briefdescription.para: computes the union of the shapes bounded by input 2d contours

detaileddescription.para:
the boundary of the union
input contours must be closed within the area of distance map and be consistently oriented (clockwise, that is leaving the bounded shapes from the left). the value of params.withSign must be true (checked with assert() inside the function)
that polyline topology should be consistently oriented Generated from function MR::contourUnion. Parameter contoursA can not be null. It is a single object. Parameter contoursB can not be null. It is a single object. Parameter params can not be null. It is a single object. Parameter offsetInside has a default argument: 0, pass a null pointer to use it. Never returns null. Returns an instance allocated on the heap! Must call MR_Polyline2_Destroy() to free it when you're done using it.

memberdef.argsstring: (const MR_Polyline2 *contoursA, const MR_Polyline2 *contoursB, const MR_ContourToDistanceMapParams *params, const float *offsetInside)
memberdef.name: MR_contourIntersection

briefdescription.para: computes the intersection of the shapes bounded by input 2d contours

detaileddescription.para:
the boundary of the intersection
input contours must be closed within the area of distance map and be consistently oriented (clockwise, that is leaving the bounded shapes from the left). the value of params.withSign must be true (checked with assert() inside the function)
that polyline topology should be consistently oriented Generated from function MR::contourIntersection. Parameter contoursA can not be null. It is a single object. Parameter contoursB can not be null. It is a single object. Parameter params can not be null. It is a single object. Parameter offsetInside has a default argument: 0.0f, pass a null pointer to use it. Never returns null. Returns an instance allocated on the heap! Must call MR_Polyline2_Destroy() to free it when you're done using it.

memberdef.argsstring: (const MR_Polyline2 *contoursA, const MR_Polyline2 *contoursB, const MR_ContourToDistanceMapParams *params, const float *offsetInside)
memberdef.name: MR_contourSubtract

briefdescription.para: computes the difference between the shapes bounded by contoursA and the shapes bounded by contoursB

detaileddescription.para:
the boundary of the difference
input contours must be closed within the area of distance map and be consistently oriented (clockwise, that is leaving the bounded shapes from the left). the value of params.withSign must be true (checked with assert() inside the function)
that polyline topology should be consistently oriented Generated from function MR::contourSubtract. Parameter contoursA can not be null. It is a single object. Parameter contoursB can not be null. It is a single object. Parameter params can not be null. It is a single object. Parameter offsetInside has a default argument: 0.0f, pass a null pointer to use it. Never returns null. Returns an instance allocated on the heap! Must call MR_Polyline2_Destroy() to free it when you're done using it.

memberdef.argsstring: (const MR_DistanceMap *distMap, const MR_AffineXf3f *toWorld, MR_PassBy cb_pass_by, MR_std_function_bool_from_float *cb)
memberdef.name: MR_distanceMapToMesh

detaileddescription.para: converts distance map into mesh and applies a transformation to all points Generated from function MR::distanceMapToMesh. Parameter distMap can not be null. It is a single object. Parameter toWorld can not be null. It is a single object. Parameter cb has a default argument: {}, pass MR_PassBy_DefaultArgument and a null pointer to use it. Never returns null. Returns an instance allocated on the heap! Must call MR_expected_MR_Mesh_std_string_Destroy() to free it when you're done using it.

export distance map to a grayscale image
threshold
- threshold of valid values [0.; 1.]. pixel with color less then threshold set invalid Generated from function MR::convertDistanceMapToImage. Parameter distMap can not be null. It is a single object. Parameter threshold has a default argument: 1.0f/255, pass a null pointer to use it. Never returns null. Returns an instance allocated on the heap! Must call MR_Image_Destroy() to free it when you're done using it.

load distance map from a grayscale image:
threshold
- threshold of valid values [0.; 1.]. pixel with color less then threshold set invalid
invert
- whether to invert values (min is white) or leave them as is (min is block) Generated from function MR::convertImageToDistanceMap. Parameter image can not be null. It is a single object. Parameter threshold has a default argument: 1.0f/255, pass a null pointer to use it. Parameter invert has a default argument: true, pass a null pointer to use it. Never returns null. Returns an instance allocated on the heap! Must call MR_expected_MR_DistanceMap_std_string_Destroy() to free it when you're done using it.
